titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Wallace Irwin Papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15952092.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday August 21, 2021 at 03:48

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract:
Correspondence, papers, and photographs by or relating to William Wallace Irwin, 1899-1959. Documented--primarily through letters addressed to Ellen Dripps Irwin--are William Irwin's brief time as an assayer in Sultan, Washington; subsequent efforts operating a cattle ranch (identified as JM Ranch) near Bear, Idaho, with F. A. Moore; ownership of a drugstore in Weiser, Idaho; work as a cost engineer in Grace, Idaho; and life in France. Photographs are primarily of Idaho.
Included is correspondence relating to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Vison on the Winlock William Miller, Jr. Memorial Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three series: I. Correspondence, 1902-1959. II. Papers, 1903-1956. III. Photographs, 1899-1913.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Wallace Irwin (1881-1973) of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, was the son of James Irwin and Ellen Dripps Irwin (1844-1930).

Irwin studied chemistry and minerology at the University of Pennsylvania before moving to Sultan, Washington, in 1902 to work as an assayer. In 1902 November, he purchased a cattle ranch near Bear, Idaho, with business partner F. A. Moore. In 1905, he purchased a drugstore in Weiser, Idaho. He began work as a cost engineer in Grace, Idaho, for the Phoenix Construction Company in 1913.

In 1918, Irwin was living in France and working for the American University Union at Paris. He also worked for the Farmers' Loan & Trust Co. and American Chamber of Commerce. In 1927, he published On the slope of Montmartre with seventeen illustrations in duotone by Marcel Poncin (New York: Frederick A. Stokes Company, 1927). He remained in the country until his death.
He was married to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin, with whom he had a son, William Wallace Irwin-d'Estrées.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Wallace Irwin Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, papers, and photographs by or relating to William Wallace Irwin, 1899-1959. Documented--primarily through letters addressed to Ellen Dripps Irwin--are William Irwin's brief time as an assayer in Sultan, Washington; subsequent efforts operating a cattle ranch (identified as JM Ranch) near Bear, Idaho, with F. A. Moore; ownership of a drugstore in Weiser, Idaho; work as a cost engineer in Grace, Idaho; and life in France. Photographs are primarily of Idaho.
Included is correspondence relating to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The William Wallace Irwin Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by sender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains circa 310 letters by or to William Wallace Irwin and wife Andrée d'Estrées Irwin.

Circa 200 letters sent from William Irwin to mother Ellen Dripps Irwin document his brief time as an assayer in Sultan, Washington; subsequent efforts operating a cattle ranch--identified as JM Ranch--near Bear, Idaho, with business partner F. A. Moore; work as a cost engineer in Grace, Idaho, with the Phoenix Construction Company; and time in France with the American University Union at Paris.
Approximately 136 letters sent from William Irwin to Ellen Irwin betweeen 1902 July and 1906 March are accompanied by typescript transcriptions prepared by dealer.
45 letters are addressed to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin--15 of these concern her writing submissions to an astrology magazine.

did.unittitle: Beebe, William to William Wallace Irwin (photocopies)
Irwin, Andrée d'Estrées to William Wallace Irwin
Irwin, Ellen Dripps to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin and family
Irwin, Ellen Dripps to William Wallace Irwin
Irwin, William Wallace to Ellen Dripps Irwin (22 folders)
Jordan, Ruth to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin
Lockwood, Priscilla to William Wallace Irwin
Moore, F. A. to William Wallace Irwin
Other letters to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin (in French)
Other letters to Andrée d'Estrées Irwin (in English)
Other letters to William Wallace Irwin (2 folders)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by content.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains papers relating to William Wallace Irwin. These include calling cards, certificates of deposit with the German American Investment Company, papers documenting Irwin's forest service in Idaho, a ledger of account between naturalist C. William Beebe and Irwin, life insurance documents, a draft of a manuscript and accompanying notes on writing, certificates of Irwin's service with the Idaho and Oregon national guards, and ranch book kept for JM Ranch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged alphabetically by content.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains 81 photographs of Alexander, Grace, and Cambridge, Idaho; the Irwin Drug Store in Weiser, Idaho; JM Ranch near Bear, Idaho; the 1905 Lewis and Clark Centennial Exposition in Portland, Oregon; and portraits of individuals (some of whom are identified), including William Wallace Irwin. Other photographs show unidentified men and women in unidentified locations.
